user_query:
I need a rigorous multi-timeframe technical analysis for my small crypto watchlist using only the OHLCV data I’ve provided locally — do not fetch any network data.

Instructions:
1) Read input/portfolio.json to get the exact symbols, timeframes, and any config flags. For each symbol+timeframe, load the matching CSV from input/ohlcv/ (column order: timestamp,open,high,low,close,volume; last row is most recent). Use ONLY these files for all calculations.

2) For each requested symbol+timeframe, compute a complete indicator set and pattern scan, then write a machine-readable JSON file with this exact schema under output/analysis/:
   - Filename: output/analysis/{SYMBOL_NO_SLASH}_{TIMEFRAME}.json (e.g., BTCUSDT_1h.json)
   - Top-level keys and required types:
     • symbol (string, e.g., "BTC/USDT")
     • timeframe (string, e.g., "1h")
     • current_price (number, last close)
     • indicators (object) containing at minimum:
       - rsi (number)
       - macd (object with numbers: macd, signal, histogram)
       - bollinger (object with numbers: upper, middle, lower)
       - atr (number)
       - adx (number)
       - mfi OR williamsR (at least one number field)
       - pivotPoints (object with numbers: pp, r1, r2, r3, s1, s2, s3)
       - fibonacci (object with numbers: level0, level236, level382, level500, level618, level786, level100)
       - keltner (object with numbers: upper, middle, lower)
       - donchian (object with numbers: upper, middle, lower)
       - vwap (number)
       - ichimoku (object with numbers: tenkan, kijun, senkouA, senkouB)
       - supertrend (object with: trend string in {"bullish","bearish","neutral"}, and numbers: upper, lower)
       - support_resistance (object with arrays: support[], resistance[] (numbers), and numbers: nearestSupport, nearestResistance)
     • patterns (array of objects; each object has: type (string), direction (string)) — include any of: hammer, shooting_star, doji, engulfing, morning_star, double_top, double_bottom, head_shoulders, inverse_head_shoulders, triangle, flag (array may be empty but must exist)
     • volume_analysis (string)
     • recommendation (object) with:
       - action (string in {"buy","sell","hold"})
       - confidence (integer 0–100)
       - stop_loss (number)
       - take_profit (number)
       - rrr (number; risk-reward ratio)
       - rationale (string; concise justification)
     • timestamp (string; ISO-like or "N/A")
   - Important: All numeric fields must be real numbers (not strings). Ensure JSON is valid and parseable.

   Use ATR-based levels for the trade plan: for a long idea, suggest stop_loss ≈ current_price - 1×ATR and take_profit anchored to a nearby resistance or a 2×ATR objective; for a short idea, invert accordingly. If the signal is hold/neutral, still compute levels and explain why.

3) Produce a cross-instrument summary and a watchlist:
   - Write output/summary.md containing:
     • A brief per-market synopsis with directional bias, key indicator highlights, notable patterns (if any), and top 3 support/resistance levels you used.
     • A short section titled "Risk Disclaimer" that includes the sentence: "This is not financial advice."
     • Explicit labels for each analyzed slice in the form "BTC/USDT (1h)", "BTC/USDT (4h)", and "ETH/USDT (4h)" so I can jump to sections quickly.
     • A short note on how you derived the stop loss and take profit from ATR and levels.
   - Write output/watchlist.csv with header:
       symbol,timeframe,bias,action,stop_loss,take_profit,nearest_support,nearest_resistance
     and one row per analyzed slice. bias should be one of bullish/bearish/neutral. action must match your JSON recommendation. Numeric columns must contain numbers.

Targets (tie exactly to my inputs):
- Create all of these JSON files:
  • output/analysis/BTCUSDT_1h.json
  • output/analysis/BTCUSDT_4h.json
  • output/analysis/ETHUSDT_4h.json
- Create output/summary.md and output/watchlist.csv as specified.

Scope & constraints:
- Do not call external services; rely only on input/ files.
- If a particular indicator cannot be computed due to data limits, include the field with a null value and explain in summary.md.
- Keep all narrative content in English.
- Create directories if they don’t exist.

Please start by reading input/portfolio.json and input/requirements.md, then the CSVs under input/ohlcv/, and produce all outputs under output/ as specified.
